row1.revenue: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a revenue of 716.9B USD, narrowly edging out Walmart Inc. (WMT) with a 3.7B USD margin. The top two companies combined account for nearly 60% of the total revenue of the top five.
row1.net_income: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a staggering $132.2B in Net Income, outpacing NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) by $12.1B. The top 3 companies (GOOGL, NVDA, AAPL) collectively account for nearly 60% of the total Net Income of the top 5.
row1.total_assets: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $4.4T in Total Assets, a significant gap of $1T from second-place Bank of America Corporation (BAC). This substantial difference underscores JPM's dominance in the banking sector.
row1.total_equity: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with $415.3B in Total Equity, outpacing Amazon.com (AMZN) by $4.2B. The top two companies hold a combined $826.4B, a significant gap from JPMorgan Chase (JPM) in third place with $362.4B.
row1.total_liabilities: JPMorgan Chase & Co. leads the pack with $4.1T in Total Liabilities, a significant gap of $1T from Bank of America Corporation's $3.1T, underscoring the massive scale of the banking sector. This gap highlights the substantial leverage in the banking industry compared to tech giants.
row1.cash_and_equivalents: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads with $343.3B in Cash And Equivalents, a significant gap from Bank of America Corporation (BAC) at $239.3B, illustrating the substantial cash reserves held by top financial institutions.
row1.gross_profit: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a Gross Profit of 360.5B USD, more than 50% ahead of Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) at 240.3B USD. This significant gap underscores AMZN's substantial scale and profitability advantage.
row1.operating_income_ebit: AAPL leads the pack with a significant Operating Income Ebit of $133.1B USD, outpacing NVDA by $2.7B USD. The top 3 companies (AAPL, NVDA, and GOOGL) collectively account for over 50% of the total Operating Income Ebit among the listed companies.

row2_sector.revenue: Consumer Cyclical and Technology sectors dominate the revenue landscape, combining for 43.1% of total revenue, while the top two sectors account for over 60% of the total.
row2_sector.total_assets: Financial Services sector dominates the sector distribution by Total Assets with 64.1% share, significantly outpacing other sectors. This concentration highlights the sector's substantial influence on the overall market.
row2_sector.net_income: Technology sector dominates the net income landscape with 39.2% share, nearly doubling the next closest sector, Communication Services at 24.8%. This significant gap highlights Technology's substantial influence on overall industry profitability.

row2_bubble: NVDA's 55.6% net margin significantly outpaces UNH's 2.7%, highlighting a substantial difference in profitability efficiency between the two companies. This disparity suggests NVDA's business model is more effective at converting revenue into net income.
row3_heatmap: Morgan Stanley (MA) stands out for its exceptional return on equity, but its peers may question the sustainability of such high returns given its relatively low leverage compared to JPMorgan Chase (JPM) with its significant debt burden.
row4_market_trends: Google (GOOGL) led the pack with a 12-month cumulative return of +83.1%, significantly outperforming the market. Conversely, Netflix (NFLX) struggled with a return of -44.3%, the worst among the highest-revenue companies. This divergence highlights the varying fortunes of tech and consumer giants.
